data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold Experiences 17% Decline, JPMorgan Sees Potential for Recovery
data.description: Gold prices have decreased by approximately 17% from their peak in January, according to a report by JPMorgan. The bank views this decline as a temporary setback. According to NS3.AI, JPMorgan suggests that an extended conflict could bolster the bullish outlook for gold.

data.title: China Restricts Manus Co-Founders Amid Meta Acquisition Scrutiny
data.description: Chinese authorities have prohibited two co-founders of Manus from leaving the country, according to a report by the Financial Times. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting increased scrutiny surrounding Meta's acquisition of the rapidly growing agentic AI startup. The move by Chinese officials comes as Meta seeks to expand its influence in the AI sector, raising concerns about international business operations and regulatory challenges. The situation underscores the complexities faced by tech companies navigating geopolitical tensions and regulatory landscapes in the pursuit of strategic acquisitions.

data.title: Thailand Lifts Diesel Price Cap Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Thailand has decided to remove its diesel price cap, a move that comes less than a month after the onset of the Middle East conflict. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the government's decision to allow market forces to dictate diesel prices. This policy shift is expected to impact both consumers and businesses as they adjust to potential price fluctuations in the energy market. The decision reflects the broader economic challenges faced by Thailand as it navigates the implications of regional instability on its energy sector. The removal of the cap is part of a larger strategy to manage the country's energy resources more effectively in response to global market dynamics.

data.title: Total CEO: Iran Conflict Halts 15% of Production, Oil Prices Offset Losses
data.description: TotalEnergies CEO Patrick Pouyanné has reported that approximately 15% of the company's total production is currently halted due to the ongoing conflict in Iran, which has persisted for nearly a month. According to Jin10, despite this disruption, the surge in oil prices has compensated for the production losses. Brent crude prices have remained above $100 per barrel, drawing significant attention to oil prices. However, Pouyanné emphasized that the crisis has had a more substantial impact on product prices, which are significantly higher than Brent crude prices. He noted that the current refining profit levels are unprecedented globally. As a major player in the global liquefied natural gas market, TotalEnergies, with its diversified global business portfolio, is able to fulfill current orders. However, if the conflict continues, natural gas prices in Europe could soar to $40 per million British thermal units this summer.

data.title: Thai Company DV8 Plans Major Bitcoin Acquisition by 2028
data.description: Thai-listed distribution company DV8 has announced its intention to acquire 10,000 BTC by the year 2028. According to NS3.AI, the company aims to purchase 1,000 BTC within this year as an initial step towards this goal. Additionally, DV8 has expressed plans to acquire the crypto custody provider Rakkar Digital, further expanding its footprint in the cryptocurrency sector.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase: The longer the conflict lasts, the stronger the reasons for being bullish on gold.
data.description: JPMorgan Chase stated in a report: "Although gold prices have fallen by about 17% from their pre-conflict January peak amid a stronger dollar and widespread risk aversion in the market, historically this has only been a temporary pullback to await buying opportunities, and the longer the conflict lasts, the stronger the bullish case becomes." (Jinshi)

data.title: Australia Temporarily Restricts Visa Holders from Iran Amid Conflict Concerns
data.description: Australia's government has announced a temporary restriction on Iranian visitor visa holders currently outside the country, according to Jin10. The decision comes amid ongoing conflicts in Iran, which have raised concerns about the ability of some temporary visa holders to leave Australia upon the expiration of their visas. The measure aims to address potential risks associated with the current geopolitical situation.

data.title: Thailand Faces Potential Decline in Foreign Visitors Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Thailand's tourism sector is facing a potential downturn as the ongoing conflict in the Middle East poses a threat to global travel. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that the number of foreign visitors to Thailand could drop to its lowest level in three years. The situation underscores the vulnerability of the tourism-dependent economy to geopolitical tensions. The decline in tourist arrivals could have significant economic implications for Thailand, which relies heavily on international tourism as a key revenue source. The government and industry stakeholders are closely monitoring the situation, hoping for a resolution that will stabilize travel patterns and support the recovery of the tourism sector.

data.title: Binance Wallet: Perle Labs (PRL) TGE participation threshold is 242 points.
data.description: According to the official announcement, Binance users with 242 or more Alpha Points can participate in the Perle Labs (PRL) TGE event through the Alpha event page. Participation in this event will consume 15 Alpha Points.

data.title: USD/JPY Reaches 159 with 0.2% Intraday Increase
data.description: The USD/JPY exchange rate has climbed to 159, marking a 0.2% increase within the day. According to ChainCatcher, this development reflects ongoing fluctuations in the currency market.

data.title: Japan's Prime Minister Requests Additional Oil Reserves Release from IEA
data.description: Japan's Prime Minister, Sanae Takaichi, has requested the International Energy Agency (IEA) to release additional oil reserves, according to Jin10. This move comes as Japan seeks to stabilize its energy supply amid global uncertainties. The request was made to IEA Director Fatih Birol, highlighting Japan's ongoing efforts to secure energy resources. The decision reflects the country's strategic approach to managing its energy needs in the face of potential disruptions.

data.title: Prediction Market Users Face Higher Losses Compared to Sports Bettors
data.description: Prediction market participants experienced a median return of -8%, which is worse than the -5% median return observed among sports bettors. According to NS3.AI, traders with a volume exceeding $500,000 managed to earn a 2.6% return. However, all users below this volume threshold incurred losses, with those trading under $100 facing a median return of -26.8%.

data.title: YZi Labs primarily invests in equity, suggesting that entrepreneurs prioritize Product-Market Fit (PMF) over rushing to issue tokens.
data.description: Ella Zhang, head of YZil Labs, stated in an article on the X platform that YZil Labs invests broadly in the Web3, AI, and Biotech sectors, with most investments made in equity rather than tokens. For portfolios that haven't yet found Product-Market Fit (PMF), she advises founders to focus on PMF rather than rushing to issue tokens. Prematurely issuing tokens often becomes a distraction and burden, and without sustainable utility, the token's value will disappear.

data.title: U.S. Senate Rejects Proposal to Limit Trump's Military Actions Against Iran
data.description: On March 24, the U.S. Senate voted against a proposal aimed at preventing U.S. President Donald Trump from initiating further military actions against Iran without congressional approval. According to Jin10, the Senate's vote resulted in 47 in favor and 53 against, leading to the rejection of the proposal. The voting largely followed party lines, with Kentucky Republican Senator Rand Paul being the only Republican to vote in favor, while Pennsylvania Democratic Senator John Fetterman was the sole Democrat to oppose the measure. This marks the third unsuccessful attempt by the Senate to pass legislation restricting Trump's authority to engage militarily with Iran since the joint military strike by the United States and Israel on Iran in late February.

data.title: A bill limiting Trump's war powers was rejected again by the US Senate.
data.description: The U.S. Senate voted on March 24 to reject a bill that would have prevented President Trump from launching further military action against Iran without congressional approval. The vote was 47-53, failing to pass. The vote was almost entirely along party lines: all Republicans voted against the bill, except for Senator Rand Paul of Kentucky; all Democrats voted in favor, except for Senator John Fetman of Pennsylvania. This is the third time the Senate has failed to pass a bill limiting Trump's power to use force against Iran since the U.S.-Israel joint military strike against Iran in late February. (CCTV)

data.title: South Korea to Invest in Advanced Aviation Technology for Defense, President Lee Jae-myung Announces
data.description: South Korean President Lee Jae-myung has announced plans to invest in advanced aviation technology to bolster the country's defense capabilities. According to Jin10, this strategic move aims to enhance South Korea's military strength amid growing regional security challenges. President Lee emphasized the importance of cutting-edge technology in maintaining national security and ensuring the country's preparedness for potential threats. The investment will focus on developing innovative aviation solutions that can provide a competitive edge in defense operations. This initiative reflects South Korea's commitment to strengthening its defense infrastructure and adapting to evolving geopolitical dynamics.

data.title: Rising Costs Impact Japan's Hanami Picnics
data.description: Cherry trees are blooming across Japan, marking the start of the traditional hanami season. Bloomberg posted on X that the cost of foods typically enjoyed during these flower-viewing picnics has reached unprecedented levels. This increase in prices is affecting the way people celebrate this cultural event, as many are reconsidering their picnic plans due to the higher expenses. The rising costs are attributed to various economic factors impacting the country, leading to a more expensive hanami season this year.

data.title: Baltimore Sues X Corp., xAI, and SpaceX Over Alleged Consumer Protection Violations
data.description: Baltimore has filed a lawsuit against X Corp., xAI, and SpaceX in a Maryland court, accusing them of violating local consumer protection laws. According to NS3.AI, the lawsuit alleges that Grok, a product associated with these companies, generated between 1.8 million and 3 million sexualized images within a few days. The city is pursuing civil penalties, injunctive relief, restitution for affected residents, and the disgorgement of profits.

data.title: Bitcoin Short Position Faces Liquidation Risk
data.description: James Wynn has reopened a 40x short position on Bitcoin, utilizing 1.31 BTC, which is valued at approximately $93,000. According to NS3.AI, this new position is approaching its liquidation threshold at a price of $71,936.49.

data.title: Ripple to Test RLUSD for Cross-Border Trade Payments in Singapore Sandbox
data.description: Ripple has announced plans to test RLUSD for cross-border trade payments within the Monetary Authority of Singapore's BLOOM sandbox. According to NS3.AI, the pilot will be conducted in collaboration with Unloq on the XRP Ledger, utilizing preset conditions like shipment verification to automatically trigger payments. Ripple aims to streamline trade finance workflows by eliminating manual checks and correspondent banking processes.
